Container.Name: PrintGuard
Container.Overview:
PrintGuard watches your 3D-printer cameras with an on-device vision model, pauses or cancels the print the moment it starts to fail, and sends a snapshot to your phone over ntfy, Telegram or Discord. No cloud, no account — camera frames never leave your server. Works with OctoPrint, Klipper (Moonraker) and Bambu Lab, and publishes every monitor to Home Assistant over MQTT.
Everything is in this one image — the streaming server is built in, so there is no second container to install. Open the WebUI, pick a mode, add a camera and printer, then create a monitor that binds them.
PrintGuard ships no authentication: anyone who can reach the WebUI can control your printers. Keep it on your LAN, or put Tailscale / Cloudflare Access / an auth proxy in front before exposing it. See the deployment guide: https://github.com/oliverbravery/PrintGuard/blob/main/docs/deployment.md

Config.Name: WebUI
Config.Description: Dashboard, engine socket and live video (HTTP)

Config.Name: Camera RTSP publish
Config.Description: Optional: only needed for cameras that push an RTSP stream into PrintGuard

Config.Name: Camera RTMP publish
Config.Description: Optional: only needed for cameras that push an RTMP stream into PrintGuard
